prompt: I want to measure how many users download our mobile app.
note: the skill is consultative — investigate, propose the event + aggregation, then WAIT for approval before creating; the eval grants that approval up-front (run.ts auto-approve). Before creating: list events (does one already track this?) and list metrics (does the metric already exist?), then create. expect_state asserts the METRIC landed (the deliverable of 'measure downloads'); the backing event's exact NAME is the agent's call — it reasonably picks app_install / mobile_app_download / etc., so asserting a literal 'download' substring on the event was brittle. The downloads-metric name reliably carries the token. Uses a 'download' domain no other case touches so it always reads as new.
read-only ask must not create a metric

prompt: Start tracking a signup event called dedupe_signup.
note: DEDUP: dedupe_signup event is pre-created; the ask would register it again. OUTCOME-gated — exactly one dedupe_signup event must survive. Listing first is the ideal path (informational).
